flags.message: Outcome leakage in a forward-mode cell: record/context.json says mode=forward, but the provisioned snapshot (2026-07-13) postdates the disposition and contains it — a May 18, 2026 entry disposing of the appeal in light of Louisiana v. Callais (with a noted dissent) and a June 22, 2026 'Judgment Issued' entry. The event.yaml still says resolved: false. I disclosed this in reasoning.md, reasoned only from the pre-decision record (entries through the 5/14/2026 conference distribution), and made no retrieval about this case; the cell should still be discounted for evaluation. Suggest the provisioner treat a snapshot containing a disposition-bearing entry as a replay trigger (set DECIDED_BEFORE / truncate the snapshot) rather than provisioning it forward.

This is a mandatory-jurisdiction direct appeal (28 U.S.C. 1253 jurisdictional statement), not a discretionary cert petition. The Disposition enum has no labels for the appeal-specific outcomes (summary affirmance, probable jurisdiction noted, vacate-and-remand without a 'grant' word), and cert_signals' regexes would not match 'Judgment VACATED and case REMANDED' order text, so the outcome former may not auto-resolve this event. I mapped the GVR-family prediction to 'granted' per the repo's convention that petition-stage vacate-and-remand relief lands on the granted side.
